course_description: This course provides a foundation in dental public health and professionalism in the practice of dentistry. The course explores principles of behaviour management in clinical care. Professional matters such as ethics, governance and regulation of dental practice in Australia will also be covered. The competency building activities of this course will provide students with learning through workshops and a series of case-based learning.

course_aims: This course aims to provide students with an understanding of communication skills, health law and ethics, inter-professional practice and information literacy.

learning_outcomes.description: Describe patient’s rights to health care and explain complaint mechanisms at practitioner and governance level.
Explain principles of responsible communication and professional use of media.
Reflect on the importance of practising with personal and professional integrity, honesty, and trustworthiness.
Analyse and make judgements on scenarios which involve patients' consent, confidentiality, and autonomy.
Apply the policies of the regulatory bodies that govern the provision of oral health services in Australia.
Critically apply the ethical principles and legal responsibilities underpinning the provision of dental care.

assessment_details.title: Ethical Scenario VLOG
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be presented with a choice of five ethical scenarios. Each student is required to individually produce a reflective response that thoughtfully engages with the scenario selected. The reflection must integrate relevant ethical concepts, theories, and frameworks discussed in class. The submission format should be a video. Students may optionally use a PowerPoint presentation to accompany their reflection. Read full task description Hide full task description

Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Penalty applied for late submission

assessment_details.title: Policy Brief
assessment_details.task_description: In this task, you are required to choose a contemporary issue and author a policy brief of 2,000 words on a contentious issue relating to either: National health law, AHPRA policies, guidelines or codes, complaint mechanisms governance level, or principles of professional use of media. You should aim your policy brief at policymakers within the Australian Government Department of Health or Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency. assessment_details.title: End of Semester Exams
assessment_details.task_description: A 1-hour exam consisting of different question types including but not limited to multiple choice, short answer questions, short essays and problem-solving covering content from Week 1 to Week 13 (inclusive). This is a MUST PASS assessment item (achieve a minimum of 50% to pass). Supplementary assessment is available for this item (in accordance with PPL Assessment Procedure Section 3 Part J). Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Week 10: 29/09/2025-05/10/2025: Feedback session Policy Brief Assessment : initial draft feedback session (max 10 mins for each student ) Learning outcomes: L06
Week 12: 13/10/2025-19/10/2025: Dentistry as a business Topic 11: Dentistry as a business Business ethics versus professional ethics. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L06

Week 1 :28/07/2025-03/08/2025: Dentistry in Australia Topic 1: Dentistry in Australia A description of the profession of dentistry within Australia and defining professionalism. Learning outcomes: L03, L05

Week 2: 04/08/2025-10/08/2025: What is Bioethics? Topic 2: What is bioethics? The ethical principles involved with dentistry and how they help inform professional practice. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L06

Week 3: 11/08/2025-17/08/2025: Regulation, AHPRA and Dental Board Topic 3: Regulation, AHPRA and Dental Board An introduction to the legal system and laws that impact dentistry. Accessing and understanding regulations Learning outcomes: L01, L05

Week 4: 18/08/2025-24/08/2025: Negligence, deception and conflict of interest Topic 4: Negligence, deception and conflict of interest Legal framework for negligence and compensation under Australian laws and risk management principles. Explores paternalistic and non-paternalistic deception. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05

Week 5: 25/08/2025-31/08/2025: Autonomy, consent and confidentiality Topic 5: Autonomy, consent and confidentiality Exploring the key concepts of autonomy, consent and confidentiality including ethical and legal considerations. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L06

Week 7: 08/09/2025-14/09/2025: Ethical issues in practice Topic 6: Ethical issues in practice Scope of practice, standard of care, duty to treat and terminating a patient/dentist relationship. Learning outcomes: L01, L05, L06
Week 8: 15/09/2025-21/09/2025: Quality control Topic 7: Quality control Evaluation of quality control: self and others. Mandatory reporting and whistle blowing. Learning outcomes: L05, L06
Week 9: 22/09/2025-28/09/2025: Ensuring practitioner health Topic 8: Ensuring practitioner health Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04

Week 13: 20/10/2025-26/10/2025: Expanding scope of dental practice Topic 12: Expanding scope of dental practice Evidence-based practice: ethics of differences in opinion versus differences in science. Learning outcomes: L04, L06
